title: Basic ADD/COPY for directory during build doesn't seem to be working
body:
/kind bug

/kind feature

**Description**

When trying to add a directory during a build, it doesn't seem to be working.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```Dockerfile
FROM python:3.7-stretch
ADD app.py requirements.txt /opt/app-root/
ADD templates/ /opt/app-root/templates/
```
1. podman build -t localhost/something:latest .

**Describe the results you received:**
```
podman run -p 5000:5000 -it --rm localhost/something:latest ls -l /opt/app-root/
total 16
-rw-rw-r--. 1 root root 4395 Apr 16 20:01 app.py
-rw-rw-r--. 1 root root  299 Feb 20 14:50 requirements.txt
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root 4096 Apr 16 20:46 templates
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expected to see the templates folder


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
```
ls -l
total 40
-rw-rw-r--. 1 thoraxe thoraxe 4395 Apr 16 16:01 app.py
-rwx------. 1 thoraxe thoraxe  319 Apr 16 16:46 Dockerfile
drwxrwxr-x. 2 thoraxe thoraxe 4096 Apr 16 16:09 __pycache__
-rw-r--r--. 1 thoraxe thoraxe 1124 Feb 20 09:56 README.md
-rw-rw-r--. 1 thoraxe thoraxe  299 Feb 20 09:50 requirements.txt
drwxr-xr-x. 2 thoraxe thoraxe 4096 Feb 20 06:41 templates
drwxr-xr-x. 2 thoraxe thoraxe 4096 Feb 20 10:28 tmp
-rw-r--r--. 1 thoraxe thoraxe  258 Feb 20 10:00 userdata.csv
drwxrwxr-x. 5 thoraxe thoraxe 4096 Feb 20 04:55 venv

ls -l templates/
total 4
-rw-r--r--. 1 thoraxe thoraxe 130 Feb 20 09:45 hello.html
```

I originally started with;

`COPY app.py requirements.txt templates/ /opt/app-root/`

And that didn't work either. Various attempts at either COPY or ADD with asterisks, slashes, and etc. all seem to have the same effect: the templates folder is not placed into the image.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.8.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
```

comments.body:
This works:

```Dockerfile
COPY app.py /opt/app-root/
COPY requirements.txt /opt/app-root/
COPY templates /opt/app-root/templates/
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Mind taking a look at this one? Must have slipped by when it was first reported
Will take a look today, slipped off my stack.

@thoraxe apologies for the delay on this one.

So I'm a bit confused, and perhaps it's just a matter of me not understanding your results.  Above you said you didn't see the `templates` directory, yet in the "results you received" section, it looks like a `templates` directory is there?

Using Podman 1.8.3-dev, it seems to work for me.  Can you confirm your results please, or more likely, straighten me out?  Thanks!

```
# cat ~/Dockerfile.dirs
FROM python:3.7-stretch
ADD app.py requirements.txt /opt/app-root/
ADD templates/ /opt/app-root/templates/

# My host dir struct:
# ls -RalF
.:
total 12
drwxr-xr-x.  3 root root   61 May 19 14:15 ./
dr-xr-x---. 23 root root 8192 May 19 13:51 ../
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root    0 May 19 13:47 app.py
-rw-r--r--.  1 root root    0 May 19 13:47 requirements.txt
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root root   40 May 19 14:01 templates/

./templates:
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root 40 May 19 14:01 ./
drwxr-xr-x. 3 root root 61 May 19 14:15 ../
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root  0 May 19 14:01 file1.txt
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root  0 May 19 14:01 file2.txt

# podman build -t tom -f ~/Dockerfile.dirs .
STEP 1: FROM python:3.7-stretch
Getting image source signatures

--> c94c3c018cb
STEP 3: ADD templates/ /opt/app-root/templates/
STEP 4: COMMIT tom
--> 532b92002f2
532b92002f2d6590bc4504e8bcdfa993f874422784ebeb58ebaa75464115ddec

# podman run  --rm tom ls -Rl /opt/app-root
/opt/app-root:
total 0
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root  0 May 19 17:47 app.py
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root  0 May 19 17:47 requirements.txt
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root 40 May 19 18:03 templates

/opt/app-root/templates:
total 0
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 May 19 18:01 file1.txt
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 May 19 18:01 file2.txt
```
@thoraxe Any answer for @TomSweeneyRedHat Otherwise we will close as `Works for me`.

I probably failed to copy/paste correctly.

That being said, I'm using 1.9.3 right now. But if I hit this problem again I'll report back.

Could you maybe throw your test case into a repo and I'll check it with 1.9.3?
